lesson.menu_desc: Present Perfect vs Past Simple -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Connecting Past and Present -- Present Perfect vs Past Simple
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 6 -- Connecting Past and Present

listenings.text: Hello Andreia, this is David Lewis from the International Robotic Surgery Congress. I am calling because the committee has just confirmed your panel, and we are very happy about it. I looked at your profile last week, and your career path is exactly what our audience needs to hear. You started in the intensive care unit more than ten years ago, and over the years you moved gradually into surgery. Since 2018 you have worked with robotic procedures at Einstein, and recently you have trained a whole new team. So far, you have assisted in hundreds of operations, and that is a remarkable number. For your talk, please connect the past with the present. Tell the audience what you did at each stage of your career, and then explain what you have achieved up to now. Keep it personal and clear. The bio you sent us last month was a great start, so just expand it. I look forward to hearing from you. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hello everyone, and thank you for having me. My name is Andreia, and I would like to share my story so far. I graduated as a nurse many years ago, and I started my career in the intensive care unit. For almost a decade I worked there, and it taught me to stay calm under pressure. In 2016 I had a real turning point, when I joined the surgical center for the first time. Two years later I moved into robotic surgery, and since then I have never looked back. Throughout this journey I have learned something new every single day. Recently I reached an important breakthrough, because I trained our first full robotic team without any outside help. So far I have assisted in more than five hundred procedures, and lately I have started to mentor younger nurses. When I look back on my path, I am proud of how far I have come. The past built me, and the present keeps me growing. Thank you very much for listening.

extra_audio.text: Andreia graduated as a nurse and started in the intensive care unit. A few years later she moved into the surgical center for the first time. In 2018 she began to work with robotic surgery. Since then she has assisted in hundreds of procedures. Recently she has trained a whole new team and started to mentor younger nurses.
